D1 OpticsL3 ApplyReference

Slow Light via EIT — Light Speeds Below Walking Pace

Electromagnetically Induced Transparency creates a steep dispersion slope dn/dω; group velocity v_g = c/(n + ω dn/dω) drops to m/s — Hau 1999: 17 m/s through Bose-Einstein condensate

A coupling laser opens a narrow transparency window in an otherwise absorbing atomic medium. The transparency is accompanied by a very steep dn/dω, which makes the group velocity tiny. Hau (1999) measured v_g = 17 m/s in ultracold sodium. Used in quantum memories and slow-light buffers.

Slide coupling-laser power.
